Co-authored-by: MaralWa <18118847+MaralWa@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent wheel scroll-down from resetting _userScrolledAway during streaming

Touchpad/precision-scroll devices fire interleaved up+down deltas in
the same gesture. Combined with the timing of programmatic ScrollToEnd,
a wheel-down event could arrive with distanceFromBottom=0 (because
ScrollToEnd just ran), immediately resetting _userScrolledAway=false
and undoing the user's scroll-up intent.

Remove the false-resetting path from OnUserWheelScroll entirely.
Re-enabling auto-scroll is now only handled by:
- ResetAutoScroll() when user sends a message
- OnScrollChanged detecting scrollbar drag to within 8px of bottom

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix hover white flash across all controls

Remove BrushTransition on Background from PART_Surface and similar main
border elements in all controls that have hover styles. The transition
caused a visible white flash on hover because it animated through an
intermediate wrong color value before the correct hover color applied.

Also moved Button/ToggleButton hover/pressed styles out of the
ControlTheme into regular Styles so all hover selectors share one
priority level — eliminating the style priority race that fed the
flash.

Affected controls: Button, ToggleButton, ComboBox, ListBox, TabControl,
Menu, StrataAiAgent, StrataAiSkill, StrataAiToolCall, StrataBudgetChip,
StrataCanvas, StrataCard, StrataComboBox, StrataCueSwitch,
StrataFileAttachment, StrataIconPicker, StrataSetting, StrataThink,
StrataTrace, StrataTurnSummary, Window

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Strata
repository.description: A modern, token-driven Avalonia UI theme for professional desktop applications. Light, Dark, and High Contrast variants with 30+ custom controls.

commit.message:
fix: prevent BringIntoView from overriding user scroll position

StrataThink.RequestExpandedReveal() called BringIntoView() whenever
IsExpanded changed to true — including programmatic changes from data
bindings during streaming. This bypassed StrataChatShell's
_userScrolledAway flag entirely, pulling the viewport to the bottom
every time a tool call expanded.

Now track whether expansion was triggered by user interaction (click
or keyboard) vs programmatic binding. Only call BringIntoView for
user-initiated expansions.

Also fix OnDetachedFromVisualTree to use RemoveHandler for
PointerWheelChangedEvent (matching AddHandler registration).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add `ReadOnlySpan<byte>` overloads for JNI member lookups (#1393)

Add UTF-8 span overloads for JNI class, method, and field lookups
that accept ReadOnlySpan<byte> parameters. Callers can pass u8 string
literals (e.g., "java/lang/Object"u8) to avoid string-to-UTF-8
marshalling overhead entirely.

New overloads (function pointers backend only):
- JniEnvironment.Types.FindClass/TryFindClass(ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.InstanceMethods.GetMethodID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.StaticMethods.GetStaticMethodID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.InstanceFields.GetFieldID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.StaticFields.GetStaticFieldID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniType constructor, Get*/GetCached* for methods, fields, constructors

Contributes to #950

* Add tests for ReadOnlySpan<byte> JNI lookup overloads

Tests cover:
- Equivalence: string vs u8 overloads return same JNI IDs
- End-to-end: constructor + method invocation via u8 path
- Cached lookups: GetCached* returns same instance
- Error handling: disposed type, class not found, invalid signature

* Remove Class.forName fallback from UTF-8 FindClass overload

The Class.forName fallback requires converting the UTF-8 span back to
a managed string and then to a Java string, which negates the
zero-alloc benefit of the UTF-8 path. Callers who need the classloader
fallback can use the existing string-based FindClass overload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix hover styles targeting Button instead of PART_Surface

Hover styles that set Background on the Button control (instead of the
template's Border#PART_Surface) are silently overridden by the default
Button ControlTheme hover, which directly targets PART_Surface. This
causes ControlHover to show instead of the intended hover color.

Fixed controls:
- Button.subtle: suppress BoxShadow and PART_Aurora leak from default hover
- Markdown link buttons: target PART_Surface for AccentSubtle hover
- StrataQuestionCard option buttons: target PART_Surface for hover/press
- StrataChatComposer send/mcp buttons: add PART_Surface overrides outside
  ControlTheme (double /template/ not allowed inside ControlThemes)
- StrataAttachmentList add button: same pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive self-healing for multi-agent groups lost on restart (#412)

## Problem
Multi-agent groups were silently lost across app restarts. The
`organization.json` on disk had:
- All groups with `IsMultiAgent=false`
- All sessions with `Role=Worker` (even orchestrator sessions)
- Multi-agent group IDs from event-diagnostics didn't exist in the file

Affected teams: PR Review Squad, MAUI - PR Reviewer, FixWeirdInfoPList,
PP- Organize, Evaluate Ortinau Skills, Implement & Challenge (6 teams
total, ~25 sessions).

## Root Cause
The self-healing in `LoadOrganization` only checked `Role ==
Orchestrator`, but all sessions defaulted to `Worker` when the org file
was stale. Chicken-and-egg: can't detect orchestrator → can't heal group
→ can't protect sessions → sessions scatter.

## Fix
Three-phase self-healing in `HealMultiAgentGroups()`:

1. **Phase 1**: Detect orchestrator sessions by name pattern
(`*-orchestrator*`) and restore `Role=Orchestrator`
2. **Phase 2**: Restore `IsMultiAgent` on groups that have orchestrator
sessions (with name matching to avoid incorrectly marking repo groups)
3. **Phase 3**: Reconstruct missing multi-agent groups from scattered
sessions by detecting team members via name prefix patterns

Also adds save verification and error logging to
`event-diagnostics.log`.

## Tests
6 new tests covering:
- Orchestrator role detection by name pattern (with suffix variants)
- Group reconstruction from scattered sessions
- Multiple scattered teams handling
- No false positives on regular session names
- No-op when groups are already correct

All 55 organization tests pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update .github/workflows/build-and-push-image.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix iOS TestFlight build: remove hardcoded signing identity, clean up plist injection (#411)

## Problem
The iOS build has been failing on TestFlight CI ([run
#23331578098](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/23331578098/job/67864110605))
with:

```
Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8): no identity found
error MSB3073: codesign --force --sign "Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8)" ... exited with code 1.
```

## Root Cause
The csproj had a post-build target (`InjectIOSPrivacyKeys`) with a
**hardcoded codesign exec** using Jakub Florkowski's signing identity.
This was part of a workaround that re-injected NS*UsageDescription
privacy keys into Info.plist after build, then re-signed the app.

## Fix
**Removed the entire PlistBuddy injection mechanism** (both
`InjectMacCatalystPrivacyKeys` and `InjectIOSPrivacyKeys` targets + the
hardcoded codesign).

The injection was a workaround for an old MAUI behavior that stripped
`NS*UsageDescription` keys from the output Info.plist. This is no longer
the case — verified with a fully clean build (`rm -rf bin/ obj/`) on
.NET 10 that the `CompileAppManifest` task in
[`dotnet/macios`](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs)
now preserves all source plist keys.

The privacy keys (`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`,
`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`) are already defined in both
`Platforms/iOS/Info.plist` and `Platforms/MacCatalyst/Info.plist` and
survive the build pipeline untouched.

## Verification
- ✅ Full clean build (bin/ + obj/ nuked) on Mac Catalyst — 0 errors
- ✅ Privacy keys present in output `.app/Contents/Info.plist` without
any injection
- ✅ Confirmed `CompileAppManifest` [source
code](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs#L105)
reads full source plist and doesn't strip NS* keys

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply security fixes to server.py

- FIX 1: Path traversal protection in /api/save-config with hostname sanitization and containment check
- FIX 2: Path traversal protection in /api/config-history-content with .bak extension and containment check
- FIX 3: SFTP path traversal protection via _validate_remote_filename helper
- FIX 4: Replace AutoAddPolicy with WarningPolicy and expose host key fingerprint
- FIX 5: Null JSON body validation on all POST endpoints
- FIX 6: Git files list validation with safe filename regex

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: register PointerWheelChanged with handledEventsToo to detect user scroll

The ScrollViewer handles PointerWheelChanged and sets Handled=true,
which prevented the += subscription from firing. This meant wheel
scroll-up during active streaming was not detected, allowing
auto-scroll to override the user's manual scroll position.

Switch to AddHandler with handledEventsToo: true (matching the
existing PointerPressedEvent subscription pattern).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: make Astro WordPress APIs client-first (#27)

* feat: unify auth bridge with fluent-wp-client patterns

- Refactor WordPressAuthBridgeConfig to extend WordPressClientConfig
- Add support for auth, authHeader, authHeaders configuration options
- Implement authResolver for custom context-aware auth resolution
- Update getActionAuth(), resolveUser(), isAuthenticated() to be async
- Use fluent-wp-client's createAuthResolver and resolveWordPressAuth patterns
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing cookie-based JWT sessions
- Update test fixtures to work with new async methods
- Update documentation with unified auth configuration examples

* feat: add getClientConfig() to auth bridge for fluent-wp-client compatibility

- Add getClientConfig() method that returns complete WordPressClientConfig
- Config can be used directly with new WordPressClient() or spread into actions
- Deprecate getActionAuth() in favor of the new unified getClientConfig()
- Keep getActionAuth() for backward compatibility
- Update WordPressAuthBridge interface with baseUrl property
- Update documentation with middleware and fluent-wp-client integration examples
- Add complete examples showing bridge usage with both package actions and fluent-wp-client
- All 79 integration tests pass

* refactor: make loaders and actions client-first

Use WordPressClient instances and request-aware client resolvers across Astro loaders and action factories. This removes duplicated auth/config plumbing so the package stays aligned with fluent-wp-client and the auth bridge.

* docs: remove migration-style wording from auth docs

* Update src/server/auth.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: finish client-first auth bridge API

Remove the legacy getActionAuth shim so the bridge resolves request auth through client-first helpers like the rest of the package. Preserve static auth fallback handling so bridge-created clients keep working across supported auth inputs.

* Update src/server/auth.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/actions/ability/delete.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/actions/ability/execute.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/actions/ability/get.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make auth bridge static fallback opt-in

Keep resolveUser and isAuthenticated focused on request-derived user state while still allowing explicit service-client fallback through getClient and getClientConfig.

* refactor: trim auth bridge fallback surface

Keep the opt-in static fallback behavior while reducing the auth bridge type surface to the API shape callers actually need.

* fix: respect auth bridge client authHeaders

Let per-call authHeaders override bridge-level authHeaders without allowing resolved auth fields to be replaced, and clean up the users action docs snippet.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: caddyconf
repository.description: create simple caddy reverse proxy configs

commit.message:
CaddyConfer v1.5.0 - Initial release

Web-based configuration generator for Caddy server reverse proxy.

Features:
- 12 accordion sections for complete Caddy config generation
- Live preview with syntax highlighting and validation
- Recommendations engine with auto-fix actions
- SSH/SFTP remote deployment (upload, validate, reload)
- Git push integration
- Certificate generation (CA + client + PFX)
- Full i18n (Swedish/English)
- Dark mode
- Inline Caddy documentation links
- Config history and versioning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: mark migration 0048 as applied to live DB

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Streamline pipeline: remove params.outdir overrides and consolidate shared processes

Task 1: Remove module-level params.outdir overrides from main.nf and all
affected module files (canoes, clamms, cnvkit, gatk-gcnv, evaluate,
feature-extraction, normalise, survivor, train, truvari). Also removed
unused outdir.mkdir() calls from main.nf and modules-evaluate.nf.

Task 2: Create modules/modules-common.nf with shared BGZIP_SORT_INDEX_VCF
and NORMALISE_CNV_QUALITY_SCORES processes parametrised by tool_annot/
caller_name and dir_suffix vals.

Task 3 (partial): Updated modules-canoes, modules-clamms, modules-xhmm,
modules-cnvkit, modules-indelible, and modules-gatk-gcnv to include from
modules-common.nf, remove duplicate process blocks, and pass the correct
caller/dir_suffix arguments in workflow calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: nf-multicaller-exomecnv
repository.description: The nf-multicaller-exomecnv workflow enables the calling of CNVs using CANOES, CLAMMS, CNVkit, Illumina's DRAGEN Germline Enrichment, GATK-gCNV and INDELIBLE, with SV merging using Truvari or SURVIVOR, and in silico Validation via a Random Forest Classifier

commit.message:
docs: create PROJECT_RULES. single source of truth for all project rulesmd

Centralizes all rules from:
- docs/UX_RULES.md (UX checklist 10 points)
- docs/ux-action-checklist.md (4-phase action checklist)
- docs/architecture.md (system design principles)
- docs/platform-organization-consolidation-plan.md
- src/lib/auth.ts (permission guards)
- src/lib/permissions.ts (permission key catalog)
- supabase/migrations/0002 (lifecycle_status enum)
- CLAUDE.md + copilot-instructions.md

Categories:
1. Stack & tech constraints
2. Auth & permission rules (guard functions, platform org model, permission keys)
3. DB & migration rules (soft delete pattern, lifecycle_status, current violations)
4. UX & component rules (4-phase checklist, components, form fields, new page checklist)
5. Message system rules
6. Architecture rules (case model, portal permissions, platform menu)
7. Absolute forbidden list (14 items)
8. Pre-implementation checklist (13 items)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(ux-rules): add mandatory soft delete + trash bin rule (UX #8)

- New rule: all destructive actions must use soft delete (lifecycle_status/deleted_at)
- Hard delete (.delete()) without soft_deleted gate is forbidden
- Trash UI required: restore button + admin-only hard delete
- undo() toast required after every soft delete
- Document current violations: deleteMembershipAction, missing cases trash UI
- Updated UX_RULES.md, CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md

- DotNetReleases: Rename Releases class to ReleasesView to avoid
  CS0542 (member name matching enclosing type)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update README with Codex CLI and Claude Code configuration guides

- Add detailed step-by-step setup for Codex CLI (config.toml)
- Add detailed step-by-step setup for Claude Code (env vars)
- Update supported models table with Anthropic Messages API routing
- Add supported clients table
- Update architecture diagram to show both clients
- Add API endpoints reference
- Add Claude Code specific troubleshooting entries
- Add advanced options section (custom port)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Anthropic Messages API passthrough and Claude model routing

- Add forwardAnthropicMessages() for direct Anthropic API passthrough
- Add Responses→Anthropic translation for Claude models (buildAnthropicRequest, forwardToAnthropicCopilot)
- Add normalizeModelName() to convert claude-sonnet-4-6 → claude-sonnet-4.6
- Route Claude models directly to Anthropic Messages API, skip Responses API
- Support /v1/messages and /messages endpoints for Claude Code compatibility
- Add model_not_supported to fallback error detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update README for --allow-raw, RBAC tools, and recent feature additions

- Add --allow-raw flag and api_raw tool (raw Kubernetes API access)
- Add RBAC read-only tools: list_rbac_bindings, list_rbac_roles, list_service_accounts
- Update tool count to 35 (19 read-only + 12 write + 3 destructive + 1 raw)
- Update list_resources description: sortBy, allNamespaces support
- Update get_logs description: follow/streaming, multi-pod prefix
- Update port_forward description: service/deployment/statefulset targets
- Update apply_resource: field validation level param
- Update delete_resource: force deletion param
- Update drain_node: force and timeout params
- Remove Known Limitations section (follow/streaming now supported)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(string): add strTruncate, and strCount utilities (#529)

- **strTruncate**: Truncate strings to maximum length with optional
suffix support
  - Preserves hard max length boundary (suffix included within limit)
  - Truncates suffix if needed to maintain max length constraint
  - Type coercion via asString(); throws for null/undefined

- **strCount**: Count non-overlapping substring occurrences
  - Returns 0 for empty search strings (prevents unbounded matches)
  - Type coercion via asString(); throws for null/undefined

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: embed tmux.conf with version check and auto-update prompt

- FireballEntityRenderer: var10→scale, var11→textureId, var12→tessellator, var13-16→uMin/uMax/vMin/vMax, var17-19→width/halfWidth/quarterHeight
- FishingBobberEntityRenderer: full rename of all 30+ locals
- GhastEntityRenderer: var4→attackFactor, var5→scaleXZ, var6→scaleY
- GiantEntityRenderer: var2→shadowRadius, var3→entityScale
- LightningEntityRenderer: full rename of all lightning bolt render locals

- MinecartEntityRenderer: full rename of render method locals
- ModelBase: var1-6→walkAnimPhase/walkSpeed/ageInTicks/headYaw/headPitch/modelScale

commit.message:
Rename varN style variables to descriptive names across client files

Rename all varN (var0-var30+) local variables and parameters to
descriptive names in:
- BetaSharp.Client/BetaSharp.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Guis/GuiAchievements.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Guis/GuiLevelLoading.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Guis/GuiScreen.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Input/MovementInputFromOptions.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Input/PlayerControllerMP.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Input/PlayerControllerSP.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Blocks/Entities/BlockEntityMobSpawnerRenderer.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Blocks/Entities/BlockEntityRenderer.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Blocks/Entities/BlockEntitySignRenderer.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Blocks/Entities/BlockEntitySpecialRenderer.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Core/Lighting.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Core/PositionTextureVertex.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Core/Tessellator.cs
- BetaSharp.Client/Rendering/Entities/ArrowEntityRenderer.cs

Key renames follow conventions: x/y/z for coordinates, blockId for
block IDs, partialTicks for animation parameter, entity/player for
entities, mouseX/mouseY for GUI inputs, scrollX/scrollY for scroll
state, etc.
